summary: Executed backend pytest for /api/v2 FITSIOMAX flows and frontend Playwright e2e on public URL. Backend passed for all requested core APIs; frontend passed role login, role-based tabs, doctor/slot calendar, appointment booking and double-book prevention, but CSV import visibility from Import tab failed repeatedly.

ui_bugs.issue: After CSV import action, imported lead is not visible in Leads view (empty state shown) even after refresh/filter/search.

integration_issues.flow: CSV/manual lead import from Import tab to Leads queue
integration_issues.issue: UI import action executes, but imported lead not observable in role queue table; likely frontend refresh/state sync or import request handling issue.

action_items: Fix Import tab -> Leads list sync so imported CSV leads are visible immediately in Leads table/filters.
Trace import API response in frontend (success/error) and ensure loadLeads uses latest dataset after import.
Re-test import with role filter and search after fix.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py is very large (>1400 lines); split v1/v2 domain routes into modules for maintainability.
Passwords are stored in plain text in v1 and v2 seed/auth flow; replace with password hashing before production.
Observed browser console hydration warning for invalid DOM child (<span> inside <option>) during appointments view; review rendered option content composition.
seed_data_creation: Created AUTO*/TEST* records via UI/API: services, doctors, slots, leads, appointments for validation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use public URL. Role login/tabs, service (admin-only), doctor slot creation, availability, appointment booking and no double-booking are working. Focus next on Import tab -> Leads table visibility mismatch.
rca of the issue: Reproduced 3 times in UI: Import action runs from /app Import tab, but imported lead cannot be found in Leads table with search/filter/refresh. Backend /api/v2/leads/import API passed in pytest, so probable root cause is frontend integration/state refresh path (loadAll/loadLeads/filter-state interaction) rather than backend import logic.
